pageTitle.value: Dianne Reeves & Song Yi Jeon - Video

pageCaption.value: South Korean jazz singer and composer Song Yi Jeon, who is known for her hypnotic voice, marked the end of her two-year collaboration with five-time Grammy winner Dianne Reeves through the Rolex mentoring programme by performing at the 2024 EFG London Jazz Festival.

WaPageTitle.value: Dianne Reeves & Song Yi Jeon - Video

metadescription.value: Through the Rolex mentoring programme, jazz singer Dianne Reeves is helping jazz composer Song Yi Jeon grow confident in her art. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Dianne Reeves and Song Yi Jeon - Video

title.value: Dianne Reeves and Song Yi Jeon

text.value: South Korean jazz singer and composer Song Yi Jeon, who is known for her hypnotic voice, marked the end of her two-year collaboration with five-time Grammy winner Dianne Reeves through the Rolex mentoring programme by performing at the 2024 EFG London Jazz Festival. Synchrony of Sound: Rhythm, Origin of All blends contemporary jazz with traditional Korean ‘Samulnori’ percussion. Reeves made a special appearance during the performance. Mentoring forms part of Rolex’s commitment to the transmission of knowledge to the next generation. It takes place alongside its support for leading arts institutions and artists around the world through the Perpetual Arts Initiative.

pageTitle.value: Youssou N’Dour and Aurelio Martínez - Video

pageCaption.value: A year’s mentorship with Youssou N’Dour gives Aurelio Martínez, from Honduras, not only an opportunity to boost his musical skills and self-confidence in the company of “the icon of African music”, but also to learn more about the historic and musical background of his African ancestors as he visits N’Dour’s continent for the first time.

pageTitle.value: Sir Colin Davis and Josep Caballé Domenech - Video

pageCaption.value: One of the world’s greatest conductors, (the late) Sir Colin Davis believed that “you don’t control an orchestra, you are allowing things to happen.” Preparation and a deep cultural sense are what a conductor needs – lessons that Sir Colin instilled into his brilliant protégé, Josep Caballé Domenech, from Spain.

WaPageTitle.value: Sir Colin Davis and Josep Caballé Domenech - Video

pageCaption.value: Indian tabla virtuoso Zakir Hussain and young American drummer Marcus Gilmore believe that creating music must be based on keeping the past but also exploring new possibilities.

pageTitle.value: Philip Glass and Pauchi Sasaki - Video

pageCaption.value: For composer Philip Glass, “music is a place”. He chose Pauchi Sasaki, from Peru, as his protégée because he was convinced she would gain most from the mentoring year. He helped her navigate not only the subtleties of composing but also the practicalities of living as a professional musician.

pageTitle.value: Kaija Saariaho and Vasco Mendonça - Video

pageCaption.value: Composing music is a solitary occupation. For Finland’s Kaija Saariaho and her protégé, Portugal’s Vasco Mendonça the mentorship delivered a rare opportunity to “enrich” each other discussing music in concert halls around Europe and in the United States.
